{{Quote|Flash! There are reports of Mandalorians in the building! Middle floors, roof. We're sealing the door, will try to hold out!|A ''Taris Holofeed'' reporter holed up in the Junavex Hotel, during the Mandalorian invasion of Taris|<ref name="invasion" />}}
The '''Junavex Hotel''' was a [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] in the [[Middle City]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] that had opened by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. In [[3963 BBY]], the Junavex Hotel became a point of action in the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Taris Siege|invasion]] of Taris as a ''[[Taris Holofeed]]'' [[Unidentified Taris Holofeed reporter|reporter]] and his staff holed up in the hotel's [[Senatorial Suite]] and provided a live report on the events of the siege. Eventually, Mandalorians breached the Junavex Hotel, breaking into the room and disrupting the transmission.

==Description==
{{Quote|The choice wasn't made from avarice—it provides a good view of the city and its construction is sound—though your correspondent will admit to some intentional irony.|A ''Taris Holofeed'' reporter, on the Junavex Hotel's Senatorial Suite|<ref name="invasion" />}}
The Junavex Hotel was a [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] in the [[Middle City]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]].<ref name="prime" /> Soundly constructed, the Junavex Hotel possessed a room called the [[Senatorial Suite]], which provided a good view of the planet's [[City/Legends|cityscape]]. The Senatorial Suite was normally off-limits to common Tarisians, reserved instead for Taris's [[Senator/Legends|senator]], who by [[3963 BBY]] was [[Haydel Goravvus]].<ref name="invasion" />

==History==
{{Quote|The Mandalorians have just landed at Highpoint, and the offices which source this Holofeed there were struck two days ago by some kind of explosive. Since that time—not counting a nervous night hiding in the back of Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium—this reporter and staff have been stationed at the Senatorial Suite of the Junavex Hotel.|A ''Taris Holofeed'' reporter|<ref name="invasion" />}}
The Junavex Hotel had become active by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|II|180|Junavex Hotel}}</ref> and remained open through the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. In [[3964 BBY]], a "[[Unidentified night desk clerk|family friend]]" of a [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] self-proclaimed industrialist going by the name [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Marn Hierogryph|Hieromarn]], who was soliciting investors for a [[Fleek eel/Legends|fleek eel]]-related business venture at the [[Upper City]] [[Market Street Outdoor Exchange]], worked as the night desk clerk at the Junavex Hotel. In an [[The Taris Holofeed: Prime Edition|edition]] of the ''[[Taris Holofeed]]'', it was stated that one could contact Hieromarn through this Junavex Hotel desk clerk.<ref name="prime" />

The next [[Standard year/Legends|year]], as the Mandalorian Wars continued to heat up between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans, the Mandalorians [[Taris Siege|invaded]] Taris. As the Mandalorians besieged and ransacked the planet, one [[Unidentified Taris Holofeed reporter|''Taris Holofeed'' reporter]] and his staff were stationed in the Junavex Hotel's Senatorial Suite—which now lay abandoned, as Senator Goravvus had gone missing—as they observed and reported on the events of the battle. The [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] provided live reports during the Mandalorian siege until the invaders finally moved into the hotel, breaking into the Senatorial Suite and disrupting his transmission.<ref name="invasion" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Junavex Hotel was first mentioned in ''[[The Taris Holofeed: Prime Edition]]'', an [[in-universe]] news report appearing on the last page of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]] that was released in [[2006]].<ref name="prime" />
